$comment: Deny-by-default dependency-license allowlist (design lock L-57 / O-7). 'allowed_licenses' are SPDX identifiers acceptable for code linked into shipped engine cores. 'dependency_licenses' maps each declared dependency name to its verified SPDX license; a dependency missing from this map counts as UNKNOWN and fails CI. 'v8' is V8 (BSD-3-Clause), now a SHIPPED runtime dependency of the in-process JS host (src/runtime/js/, issue #76 / L-61): acquired outside vcpkg via the SHA-pinned denoland/rusty_v8 prebuilt channel (tools/v8-prebuilt.json + tools/fetch_v8.py; the wgpu-native/CEF third-party-prebuilt precedent in docs/signing.md — TLS + SHA-pin + verify-before-use, out of scope for the first-party R-SEC-009 trust root). The rusty_v8 static archive bundles V8 (BSD-3-Clause) + Chromium libc++ (Apache-2.0 WITH LLVM-exception, allowlisted) statically; 'rusty_v8' is the MIT-licensed Rust binding project whose extern-"C" shim layer (v8__*) the hybrid host consumes for the two STL-crossing ops. (The earlier v8-v143-x64 13.0.245.25 NuGet monolith was the js-engine M0 SPIKE's throwaway stand-in — see the recorded L-42 deviation in spikes/js-engine/FINDINGS.md.) A from-source owned vcpkg V8 port is a separate pre-1.0 hardening task (src/runtime/js/README.md § From-source migration seam). 'cef' is the cef-compositing M0 spike's pinned binary distribution (149.0.6+g0d0eeb6+chromium-149.0.7827.201, Spotify CDN) — same spike-only, outside-vcpkg treatment; CEF itself is BSD-3-Clause and the distribution bundles Chromium/third-party notices in CREDITS.html (a shipped-editor CEF integration needs a fuller third-party notice pass — an M5 concern, not M0). 'wgpu-native' is the webgpu M0 spike's prebuilt GitHub-release binary (v29.0.1.1) — likewise spike-only, never shipped, acquired outside vcpkg (no vcpkg port exists); dual-licensed 'MIT OR Apache-2.0', we elect Apache-2.0; its bundled webgpu.h/wgpu.h headers are BSD-3-Clause (webgpu-headers, also allowlisted); the recorded L-42 deviation lives in spikes/webgpu/FINDINGS.md. 'wasmtime' (prebuilt C API 46.0.1) and 'wasm-micro-runtime' (WAMR 2.4.5, built from a pinned source tarball; its 'wamrc' AOT compiler prebuilt is the same project/license) are the wasm M0 spike's runtimes — likewise spike-only, acquired outside vcpkg, recorded for clearance; deviations in spikes/wasm/FINDINGS.md. 'esbuild' (MIT) is the M3 TypeScript toolchain's transpiler/bundler (src/runtime/ts/, issue #83 / R-LANG-002): a build-TIME native binary acquired outside vcpkg via the SHA-pinned esbuild-own-npm-package channel (tools/ts-toolchain.json + tools/fetch_esbuild.py; the SAME wgpu-native/CEF/rusty_v8 third-party-prebuilt precedent — TLS + SHA-pin + verify-before-use, out of scope for the first-party R-SEC-009 trust root). Unlike rusty_v8, esbuild is NEVER linked into the shipped engine (it is invoked as a subprocess), so this is a build-tool clearance entry, recorded for provenance the same way the spike runtimes are. 'glslang', 'spirv-cross' and 'spirv-tools' are the native shader cross-compile toolchain added behind the opt-in CONTEXT_BUILD_SHADER_CROSSCOMPILE path (R-REND-005, issue #125, sub-task B of #119) via the sanctioned vcpkg channel (VCPKG_MANIFEST_FEATURES=shader-crosscompile); like the wgpu backend the path is CI-gated and never resolved by the default build. 'tint' and 'naga' are that same flag-gated path's WGSL leg (issue #133, sub-task D of #119 — measured ruling in docs/wgsl-tool-decision.md): both are standalone CLIs invoked as SUBPROCESSES, never linked into the engine (the esbuild build-tool precedent). 'tint' (google/dawn — the WIRED SPIR-V->WGSL tool) is BSD-3-Clause, built FROM SOURCE at the Dawn tag pinned in tools/tint-toolchain.json (publisher TLS + pinned-commit verify-before-build, fail-closed; no official standalone prebuilts exist). 'naga' (gfx-rs/wgpu's naga-cli — the roundtrip test's native-consumer cross-validator + the evaluation-harness alternative) is dual-licensed 'MIT OR Apache-2.0' — we elect Apache-2.0, matching the wgpu-native election; installed from source via cargo (tools/naga-toolchain.json + tools/fetch_naga.py; crates.io checksums enforced by cargo itself). 'spirv-cross' (KhronosGroup/SPIRV-Cross) and 'spirv-tools' (KhronosGroup/SPIRV-Tools) are Apache-2.0. 'glslang' (KhronosGroup/glslang) is the multi-licensed SPDX expression 'Apache-2.0 AND BSD-3-Clause AND MIT AND GPL-3.0-or-later' (per the microsoft/vcpkg port manifest); every conjunct EXCEPT the GPL-3.0-or-later is already allowlisted (Apache-2.0/BSD-3-Clause/MIT). The GPL-3.0-or-later conjunct is SOLELY the preprocessor's Bison-generated parser skeleton, which carries the GNU Bison runtime 'special exception' ('you may create a larger work that contains part or all of the Bison parser skeleton and distribute that work under terms of your choice, so long as that work isn't itself a parser generator') — it therefore imposes NO copyleft on the linked engine, and the license the shipped/linked glslang runtime is governed by is permissive. We record 'Apache-2.0' as glslang's governing SPDX id (the deny-by-default map stores one id per dep, and all of glslang's non-exception conjuncts are allowlisted); this mirrors the Vulkan-SDK/industry precedent of shipping glslang under its permissive terms. Notes: blake3 is dual-licensed 'CC0-1.0 OR Apache-2.0' — we elect Apache-2.0; 'Apache-2.0 WITH LLVM-exception' is a valid SPDX expression whose exception only ADDS permissions relative to plain Apache-2.0 (waives Section-4 notice duties for compiled-binary redistribution), so allowlisting it is strictly no-riskier than Apache-2.0 itself.
